title: Template literals should not be nested
description: Template literals, also known as template strings, allow for string interpolation and multiline strings in JavaScript. They provide a more convenient and flexible way to work with strings compared to traditional string concatenation or manipulation. Template literals are delimited with the backtick (`) character.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: Nested template literals detected - consider refactoring for better readability
